steps.title: Apply the ecosystem approach
steps.content: The ecosystem-based approach considers interactions among climate, terrain, and species in a whole geographical area. The passage says this approach may lead to better outcomes for all species in that location, not just one target species.

steps.title: Keep the outcome broad
steps.content: A invents a focus on reducing competing species. C shifts to the distribution of the entire genus throughout Mexico, which is broader than the Guerrero ecosystem at issue. D says the approach would be unable to evaluate survival elsewhere, a limitation not supported by the passage. The remaining choice captures a broad strategy that still benefits M. guerreronis. The answer is B.
